merger_name: North American Construction Group – Iron Mine Contracting and Iron Hire

anzsic_codes.name: Other Heavy and Civil Engineering Construction
Heavy Machinery and Scaffolding Rental and Hiring
Other Machinery and Equipment Repair and Maintenance

merger_description:
North American Construction Group Limited (NACG), via NACG WA Acquisition Pty Ltd, proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in DCL Corp Pty Ltd, trading as Iron Mining Contracting, and Iron Hire Pty Ltd (the Targets) through a share purchase agreement.

NACG is a Canadian supplier of mining and heavy civil construction services to customers in the resource development, industrial construction and infrastructure sectors in Canada, the US, and Australia. Through its Australian subsidiaries, NACG supplies:

* heavy equipment rental services to mine operators with operators (wet hire) and without operators (dry hire) in New South Wales and Queensland;
* dry hire heavy equipment rentals in Western Australia;
* mining services to its heavy equipment rental customers in New South Wales and Queensland; and
* recycled and refurbished mining and construction equipment across Australia and worldwide.

The Targets supply mining services and civil construction services in Western Australia.

The parties both supply mining and civil construction services for large (Tier 1) and medium (Tier 2) scale mining projects.

events.title: North American Construction Group – Iron Mine Contracting and Iron Hire - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
The proposed acquisition by North American Construction Group
Limited (NACG), via NACG WA Acquisition Pty Ltd, of 100% of the
shares in DCL Corp Pty Ltd, trading as Iron Mining Contracting, and
Iron Hire Pty Ltd (together, the Targets) through a share purchase
agreement (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, NACG, is a Canadian supplier of mining and civil
construction services to customers in the resource development,
industrial construction and infrastructure sectors in Canada, the US,
and Australia. Through its Australian subsidiaries, NACG supplies:
• heavy equipment rental services to mine operators with
operators (wet hire) and without operators (dry hire) in New
South Wales and Queensland;
• dry hire heavy equipment rentals in Western Australia;
• mining and civil construction services to its heavy
equipment rental customers in New South Wales and
Queensland; and
• recycled and refurbished mining and construction
equipment across Australia and worldwide.
The Targets supply mining services and civil construction services in
Western Australia, as well as crushing and screening services.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
The Parties overlap in the supply of a range of mining and civil
construction services to large (Tier 1) and medium (Tier 2) mining
projects in Australia (Mining Services). Mining Services may involve
contract mining, civil earthmoving, project management, and
stockpile management.
NACG supplies Mining Services in Queensland and New South
Wales, only in conjunction with the supply of heavy equipment
rentals. The Targets supply Mining Services in Western Australia.
There are no current vertical relationships between the Parties,
however we understand that suppliers of Mining Services may rent
heavy equipment in the course of supplying Mining Services.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information provided by third parties and publicly
available sources.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• while both Parties supply Mining Services, they supply these
services in different regions, and would likely continue to face
competition from significant alternative suppliers of Mining
Services;
• the Parties would likely continue to face competition from
significant alternative suppliers of heavy equipment rentals;
• the Parties would be unlikely to have an ability to engage in an
anti-competitive bundling or tying strategy, given the presence of
significant alternative suppliers of Mining Services and heavy
equipment rentals;
• the Parties would be unlikely to have an ability to foreclose rivals’
access to either Mining Services or heavy equipment rentals,
given the presence of significant alternative suppliers of both
Mining Services and heavy equipment rentals.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
